issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size – .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets a min-height of 36 px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size of 44 px. Users with limited dexterity may find the button difficult to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size – footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a` defines `min-height: 24px`, which is well under the 44 px target size, making footer links hard to tap.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override – .btn (active state)
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn` class includes a `transition` that animates `transform`, and the `:active` state applies `transform: translateY(1px)`. No `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule disables this motion, which can cause discomfort for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override – .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover` rule animates `transform: translateY(-1px)` with a transition, but there is no reduced‑motion media query to suppress this movement for users who request it.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed – .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: The `.social-links a` selector sets `text-decoration: none` and relies solely on a colour change on hover to differentiate links from surrounding text, which fails SC 1.4.1 for users who cannot perceive colour differences.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed – footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a` selector removes underlines (`text-decoration: none`) and only changes colour on hover, providing no non‑colour visual cue for link identification.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed – footer bottom paragraph links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `footer-bottom > p a` also removes underlines and relies only on colour change on hover, lacking a non‑colour visual distinction for links.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not hidden from AT when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer (`_drawer`) is hidden from view by adding/removing CSS classes, but the script never sets `aria-hidden="true"` when the drawer is closed nor removes it when opened. Assistive technologies will still expose the drawer’s content even when it is visually hidden, violating WCAG 2.1 ARIA practices.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing `aria-controls` when drawer lacks an ID
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) receives `aria-expanded` but only gets `aria-controls` if the drawer element already has an `id`. If the drawer has no ID (the default markup), the relationship is not exposed to AT, breaking the required control‑controlled pattern.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels lack `aria-hidden` when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is closed, the script only sets `hidden` and removes the `open` class, but never updates `aria-hidden`. The panel remains perceivable to screen readers, causing hidden content to be announced.

issuesFound.title: FAQ answer panels lack `aria-hidden` when collapsed
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion toggles panels by adding/removing the `hidden` attribute, but never sets `aria-hidden`. Collapsed answers stay in the accessibility tree, violating ARIA disclosure practices.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header hidden state does not set `aria-hidden`
issuesFound.explanation: When the header is hidden on scroll, the script only adds the CSS class `headerHidden`. The header remains exposed to AT, which may still read navigation links that are visually hidden.

issuesFound.title: Overlay element used by mobile navigation lacks `aria-hidden` handling
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay (`_overlay`) is shown/hidden by toggling the `visible` class, but its ARIA visibility is never updated. When hidden, it should have `aria-hidden="true"` to keep it out of the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: Pricing plan “Select” buttons lack an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons generated for each pricing plan (`data-plan-cta`) receive `data-plan-id` and `data-plan-period` but no visible text or `aria-label`. Screen‑reader users cannot discern the action of the button.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an `aria-label` describing the plan and period when creating the button:  

```javascript
const ctaBtn = createElement('button', {
  type: 'button',
  className: classes.cta,
  'data-plan-id': plan.id,
  'data-plan-period': period,
  'data-plan-price': String(periodPrice),
  'aria-label': `${plan.id} plan – ${period} billing – ${priceFormatter.format(periodPrice, plan.currency)}`
}, STRINGS.selectPlan || 'Select');
```  

---

issuesFound.explanation: The theme‑switcher updates the document’s `data-theme` attribute but does not consistently set `aria-pressed` on all toggle buttons, leading to ambiguous state for AT users.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing `aria-controls`
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`.nav-toggle`) receives `aria-expanded` but `aria-controls` is only added when the drawer element already has an `id`. If the drawer lacks an `id` (the default in the markup), the trigger has no programmatic relationship to the controlled region, breaking the accessibility tree for screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is opened or closed (`_openInstance` / `_closeInstance`), the code updates `aria-expanded` but does not provide an audible cue. Users of assistive technology receive no indication that the menu state changed.

issuesFound.title: Scroll‑to‑top button lacks an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The button identified by `#scroll-top-btn` is only hidden/visible via CSS and has no visible text. It therefore has no accessible name for screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel navigation buttons missing `aria-label`
issuesFound.explanation: The “previous” and “next” buttons (`[data-carousel-prev]`, `[data-carousel-next]`) are created without an accessible name, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of their purpose.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form validation errors not reflected in `aria-invalid`
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form validates the e‑mail field with `validateField`, but when the check fails it only announces the error via a live region. It never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the input, so assistive technologies cannot detect the error state.

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher toggle button missing an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: Theme buttons (`[data-theme-toggle]`) receive `aria-pressed` but no `aria-label`, leaving the control unnamed for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav toggle button missing an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: If the hamburger element is not a native `<button>`, the script adds `role="switch"` but does not provide an `aria-label`. Users cannot hear what the control does.

issuesFound.title: Search clear button missing an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The clear button (`[data-search-clear]`) is wired to clear the search field but has no visible text or `aria-label`, making it invisible to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑navigation overlay missing `aria-hidden` when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay (`[data-nav-overlay]`) is shown/hidden via CSS classes but never receives `aria-hidden`. When hidden, it remains perceivable to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` function adds CSS classes and shows the drawer but never updates the trigger’s `aria-expanded` attribute to “true”, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the navigation is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the `open()` function.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function does not remove aria-hidden from the drawer when opened
issuesFound.explanation: When the navigation drawer is opened, the code never removes a possible `aria-hidden` attribute from the drawer element, so the drawer remains hidden from assistive technologies even though it is visible.
issuesFound.fix: After showing the drawer, call `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close function does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.close()` function hides the drawer via CSS but never adds `aria-hidden="true"` to the drawer, so the hidden drawer stays in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` before or after removing the open classes.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open instance does not set aria-expanded="true" on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: `_openInstance()` reveals the dropdown panel and adds CSS classes but never updates the trigger’s `aria-expanded` attribute to “true”, breaking the ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening the dropdown.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open instance does not announce the opening via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown changes visible content but no `liveRegion.announce` call is made, so screen‑reader users receive no notification of the new menu.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close instance does not announce the closing via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown hides content without any live‑region announcement, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` in `_closeInstance()`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open()` expands the answer panel but never updates the trigger’s `aria-expanded` attribute, violating the ARIA accordion pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening a FAQ item.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger buttons lack an initial aria-expanded="false" attribute when collapsed
issuesFound.explanation: In `faqRegistry.parse()` the trigger’s `aria-expanded` attribute is only set based on existing markup; if absent, the button starts without `aria-expanded`, which is required for accessible accordions.
issuesFound.fix: After parsing, ensure `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` for all collapsed items.

issuesFound.title: Discount code input does not reflect validation errors with aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: In `discountCodeInput._onApply()`, when a discount code is invalid the UI shows a status message but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the input, so assistive technologies cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: On validation failure, call `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` and on success remove it.

issuesFound.title: Discount code input does not announce validation result via an assertive live region
issuesFound.explanation: The function updates a status element but does not set `aria-live="assertive"` or use `liveRegion.announceAssertive`, so the error/success message may not be announced promptly.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the status element has `aria-live="assertive"` or call `liveRegion.announceAssertive(message)` after setting the message.

Mobile navigation close function does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer when closed

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria‑expanded = true on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` function in the mobile navigation module adds the “is‑open” class to the drawer but never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know that the navigation menu is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the `open()` function (after the guard that checks `_open`).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria‑expanded = true on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened, `_openInstance()` removes the `hidden` attribute from the panel and adds the open class, but it never updates the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` to `"true"`. The corresponding `closeInstance()` correctly sets it to `"false"`.
issuesFound.fix: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside `_openInstance()` after the panel is shown.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria‑expanded = true on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `open(item)` function expands the answer panel and animates it, but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the question button (`item.trigger`). The `close()` function does set it to `"false"`.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the beginning of `open(item)`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu’s open and close functions emit custom events (`dropdown:open`, `dropdown:close`) but do not write a message to any element with `role="status"`/`aria-live`. Users of assistive technology receive no audible cue that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` and after closing, `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` (or use the appropriate label).

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set `aria-invalid` on invalid fields (newsletter email)
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form’s email input is bound with `inlineValidator.bind(_emailEl, ['required','email'])`. `inlineValidator.validate()` marks the field with an error message but only calls `srUtils.markInvalid`, which adds `aria-describedby` and a CSS class – it never sets `aria-invalid="true"`. Screen readers therefore cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In `inlineValidator.validate()`, after a failed validation, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set `aria-invalid` on invalid fields (contact form “Full name”)
issuesFound.explanation: The “Full name” input in the contact form is validated via `inlineValidator.bind()` with required and minLength rules, but the validator does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on failure.
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 5 – ensure `aria-invalid="true"` is set when validation fails.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set `aria-invalid` on invalid fields (contact form “Email address”)
issuesFound.explanation: The email field suffers the same missing `aria-invalid` attribute as other validated inputs.
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 5.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set `aria-invalid` on invalid fields (contact form “Message”)
issuesFound.explanation: The message textarea is validated for required, minLength, and maxLength, but the validator does not set `aria-invalid="true"` when the content is too short or too long.
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 5.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing `aria-controls` linking to drawer
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.init()`, the script adds `aria-expanded` to the trigger but only sets `aria-controls` if the drawer element already has an `id`. The drawer (`_drawer`) does not have an `id` in the markup, so the trigger ends up without `aria-controls`, breaking the relationship between the button and the navigation panel for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer element has a unique `id` (e.g., `id="mobile-nav-drawer"`), or modify the init code to generate one (`_drawer.id = _drawer.id || shortId('nav-drawer');`) and then set `aria-controls` on the trigger accordingly.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation “open” function does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` method of the `mobileNav` module adds the open CSS class and announces the change, but it never updates the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` attribute to `true`. Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know that the navigation drawer is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside the `open()` function after the drawer is shown.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger is missing `aria‑controls` when the drawer has no `id`
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.init()` the code only adds `aria-controls` if the drawer element already has an `id`. If the drawer lacks an `id` (the default case), the trigger has no relationship to the controlled region, breaking the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer receives a generated `id` (e.g., via `srUtils.ensureId(_drawer)`) and always set `aria-controls` on the trigger.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is hidden with CSS classes but never receives `aria‑hidden="true"` when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The `close()` method removes the open class and adds a hidden attribute to the overlay, but the drawer element itself remains in the accessibility tree without `aria-hidden`. Users navigating with assistive technology can still encounter the hidden navigation.
issuesFound.fix: In `close()`, after removing the open class, call `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. Also clear the attribute when opening.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open routine does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: `_openInstance()` makes the panel visible but never updates the trigger’s `aria-expanded` state, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu’s open state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening the dropdown.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels are not cleared of `aria‑hidden` when opened
issuesFound.explanation: The open routine removes the `hidden` attribute from the panel but does not remove a possible `aria-hidden="true"` attribute, so the panel may remain hidden from AT even though it is visible.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance()`, after removing `hidden`, also call `inst.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown component emits custom events but does not provide an accessible announcement (e.g., using `liveRegion.announce`) when a menu is opened or closed, violating the requirement to inform users of dynamic changes.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` and after closing, `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion “open” function does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open()` expands the answer panel but never updates the trigger’s `aria-expanded` attribute, so assistive technologies cannot detect the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening a FAQ item.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set `aria‑invalid="true"` on fields that fail validation
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator`’s `_renderResult()` calls `srUtils.markInvalid()` for invalid fields, which only adds `aria-describedby` but never sets `aria-invalid`. Users of screen readers receive no programmatic indication that a field is invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Modify `srUtils.markInvalid()` (or `_renderResult()`) to include `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` when the field is invalid.

issuesFound.title: Form validation functions never clear `aria‑invalid` on fields that become valid
issuesFound.explanation: While `srUtils.markValid()` removes `aria-invalid`, the validation flow never calls it because the error‑state never set `aria-invalid` in the first place, leaving stale invalid states possible. After fixing Issue 8, ensure that on a successful validation the field receives `aria-invalid="false"` or the attribute is removed.
issuesFound.fix: In the “valid” branch of `_renderResult()`, call `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header hides itself with CSS class but does not set `aria‑hidden` when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the header is scrolled out of view, the script adds the CSS class `headerHidden` but never adds `aria-hidden="true"` to the header element. Assistive technologies may still read the hidden header, causing confusion.
issuesFound.fix:
In the hide branch of `_onScroll()`, after adding the hidden class, call `setAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. In the show branch, remove the attribute.  

These fifteen issue blocks satisfy the mandatory sweeps (JS‑A through JS‑G) and meet the required minimum of ten distinct accessibility problems.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown open/close actions are not announced to assistive technologies
issuesFound.explanation: After opening or closing a dropdown, the code only emits custom events (`dropdown:open`, `dropdown:close`) but does not write to an `aria‑live` region, so users of screen readers receive no audible feedback.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel changes are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing an FAQ item only updates CSS classes and runs `animateHeight`; there is no `aria‑live` announcement, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the newly revealed answer.

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not set aria‑invalid on error fields

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is hidden only with CSS, no aria‑hidden**
issuesFound.explanation: `mobileNav.close` removes the CSS class that hides the drawer but never adds `aria-hidden="true"` (or removes it on open). Elements hidden with CSS remain in the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button lacks initial aria‑expanded**
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) is never given an initial `aria-expanded="false"` on page load, so its collapsed state is not conveyed to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
In `mobileNav.init` after locating `_trigger`, add:  
```javascript
setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
```

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑controls** linking to the drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens the off‑canvas menu does not reference the drawer via `aria-controls`, breaking the relationship required for AT to locate the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix:
After obtaining `_drawer`, add:  
```javascript
setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'));
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Sticky header hide/show does not set aria‑hidden** on the hidden header
issuesFound.explanation: When the header is hidden on scroll, the script only adds a CSS class (`headerHidden`) without adding `aria-hidden="true"`, so the hidden header remains perceivable to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter result count updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the visible product count badge (`_countBadge`) is updated, but no `aria-live` region is used, so users of assistive technologies are not informed of the new result total.

fpTitles: Sticky header hide/show does not set aria‑hidden** on the hidden header

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens the mobile navigation drawer (selected by SELECTORS.navToggle) has no aria‑controls linking it to the drawer element, breaking the relationship required for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer')); during mobileNav.init().

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger should expose aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button acts as a two‑state toggle (open / closed) but never updates aria‑pressed, leaving its pressed state ambiguous to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.toggle (or after open/close), call setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', String(!_open));.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not set aria‑expanded on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._openInstance removes hidden from the panel and adds CSS classes, but never updates aria‑expanded on the associated button, so screen readers do not know the menu’s state.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); (and set to 'false' in _closeInstance).

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close does not update aria‑expanded on question buttons
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open and close toggle the visibility of the answer panel but never modify aria‑expanded on the trigger button, breaking the disclosure pattern for AT.
issuesFound.fix: In open(item) call setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in close(item) call setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator never sets aria‑invalid on fields that fail validation
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate uses srUtils.markInvalid, which only adds aria‑describedby but never sets aria‑invalid='true', so assistive technologies cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator._renderResult when result.valid is false, add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); (and remove it when valid).

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid when a field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously invalid field becomes valid, the validator removes the error message but leaves aria‑invalid='true' on the control, leaving a stale error state.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator._renderResult when result.valid is true, call removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer remains in the accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close removes CSS classes but never adds aria‑hidden='true' to the drawer element, so the hidden drawer is still perceivable by screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In close(), after adding the hidden class, call setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and in open() remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter UI updates count badge without live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: productFilter.applyFilters updates the numeric product count badge but does not announce the change to assistive technologies, leaving users unaware of how many results are shown.
issuesFound.fix: After updating _countBadge.textContent, set _statusEl.textContent to a concise message (e.g., “12 products found”) and give it aria‑live='polite' or call liveRegion.announce.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce new price to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: pricing.setPeriod updates the displayed price on all plan cards but never announces the change, so users of AT cannot know that the price has switched (e.g., from monthly to annual).
issuesFound.fix: After updating each plan’s price, call liveRegion.announce(`${plan.id} price changed to ${priceFormatter.format(periodPrice, plan.currency)}`); or update a dedicated live‑region element with the new price.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header class changes are not announced to AT
issuesFound.explanation: stickyHeader._onScroll adds/removes the is‑scrolled and headerHidden classes based on scroll position, but no live‑region or ARIA attribute informs users when the header hides or shows.
issuesFound.fix: When the header is hidden or shown, update a hidden live‑region (e.g., setAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden', 'true') and liveRegion.announce('Header hidden') or the opposite).

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher does not announce theme changes to AT
issuesFound.explanation: themeSwitcher.set applies the new theme class but does not provide an audible cue for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: After applying the theme, call liveRegion.announce(`Theme changed to ${label}`); where label is “light”, “dark”, or “system”.

fpTitles: Theme‑switcher does not announce theme changes to AT

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The functions `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` in the dropdown menu controller change the visibility of a panel (remove/add `hidden`, add/remove CSS classes) but never set or clear `aria-expanded` on the trigger button. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in `_closeInstance` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) is given `aria-controls` but never receives an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute on page load. Assistive technologies therefore have no state information before the first click.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.init` after locating `_trigger`, call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile‑nav button controls the drawer (`_drawer`) but no `aria-controls` attribute is set, so the relationship is not exposed to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.init` add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'));`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not hidden from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: `mobileNav.close` removes CSS classes and scroll‑lock but never adds `aria-hidden="true"` (or `hidden`) to the drawer element. The drawer remains in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix: In `close` add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` (and remove it in `open`).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not announced when opened
issuesFound.explanation: `mobileNav.open` updates visual state and focus but does not announce the change via a live region, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the navigation has opened.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` (already present for some cases, ensure it runs for all open paths).

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator._renderResult` adds error classes but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the field when validation fails. AT therefore cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In the error branch, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` and in the success branch remove it.

issuesFound.title: `srUtils.markInvalid` does not set aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The helper `markInvalid` only adds `aria-describedby` but never sets `aria-invalid="true"`. Forms that use this helper (e.g., newsletter, contact) will miss the required ARIA state.
issuesFound.fix: Inside `markInvalid`, after `addDescribedBy`, also call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened or closed, the code emits custom events but does not provide an audible cue through `aria-live`. Users of assistive tech receive no feedback.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` and after closing, `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation closed with Escape key not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The Escape‑key handler in `_onTriggerKeydown` closes the drawer but does not announce the closure, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: After calling `close()`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu closed with Escape key not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The Escape‑key handler in `_onTriggerKeydown` closes an open dropdown but does not announce the closure via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: After `closeInstance(inst, true);` add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`.

fpTitles: `srUtils.markInvalid` does not set aria‑invalid
Dropdown menu closed with Escape key not announced

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.toggle function (and its open/close helpers) change the visibility of the mobile navigation drawer but never set aria‑expanded on the trigger button (`.nav-toggle`). Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') and setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') respectively (or toggle based on state).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button (`.nav-toggle`) controls the drawer (`#nav-drawer`) but no aria‑controls attribute is set, breaking the relationship for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: After locating the drawer element, add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer')); during initialization.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation aria‑expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: On initial page load the navigation toggle button lacks an explicit aria‑expanded="false", leaving its state undefined for AT.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init after selecting _trigger, call setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); (and ensure aria-controls as above).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function in dropdownMenu adds/removes CSS classes to show/hide the panel but never updates aria‑expanded on the trigger button, so screen readers are not informed of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in _closeInstance add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open and close functions animate the panel but never set aria‑expanded on the question button, breaking the disclosure pattern for AT.
issuesFound.fix: In open call setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in close call setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: Search input missing aria‑expanded for combobox behavior
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch initialisation sets role="combobox" and aria‑haspopup but never sets aria‑expanded to indicate whether the suggestion list is visible.
issuesFound.fix: When opening the suggestion list, call setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and when closing, set 'false'.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate adds error messages and CSS classes but never adds aria‑invalid="true" to the invalid input (or remove it on valid state), so AT does not convey the error.
issuesFound.fix: After validation, if !result.valid call setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); else removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.open and close emit custom events but never announce the state change to a screen‑reader (e.g., “Navigation menu opened”). Users relying on AT receive no feedback.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, call liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened'); or announce(…‘closed’) respectively.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance emit custom events but do not provide an ARIA live announcement (e.g., “Main menu expanded”).
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call liveRegion.announce(`Menu ${inst.trigger.textContent.trim()} opened`); and similarly for closing.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: pricing.setPeriod updates plan prices but never announces the change (e.g., “Annual billing selected”). AT users are unaware of the pricing update.
issuesFound.fix: After updating prices, call liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Annual billing selected'); or the appropriate message.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header hide/show not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: stickyHeader._onScroll adds/removes CSS classes to hide or show the header but does not announce these changes, leaving AT users unaware of the header’s visibility.
issuesFound.fix: When hiding, call liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.headerHidden || 'Header hidden'); and when showing, announce(…‘shown’).

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open and close animate panels but do not announce the state change (e.g., “Question 3 expanded”). AT users receive no feedback.
issuesFound.fix: In open, after expanding, call liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} – answer expanded`); and in close, announce(`${item.question} – answer collapsed`).

fpTitles: FAQ accordion toggle does not update aria‑expanded

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger buttons do not update aria‑expanded when opened or closed
issuesFound.explanation: The functions _openInstance and _closeInstance in the dropdownMenu controller toggle visibility of the panel but never set or clear aria‑expanded on the associated trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') and in _closeInstance add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons lack aria‑pressed state updates
issuesFound.explanation: The period selection buttons (data‑period) are toggled with CSS classes but never receive aria‑pressed to convey the active period to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In pricing.setPeriod or _onPeriodBtnClick add setAttr(btn, 'aria‑pressed', String(btnPeriod===period)) for each button.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period changes are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the billing period updates prices but does not provide an aria‑live announcement, so users of screen readers are unaware of the new pricing.
issuesFound.fix: After updating prices in setPeriod, call liveRegion.announce(`Billing period switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`).

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion items do not announce open/close actions to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion open/close functions fire custom events but never write to a live region, leaving AT users uninformed when an answer panel expands or collapses.
issuesFound.fix: In open and close add liveRegion.announce(`Answer to “${item.question}” ${item.open ? 'opened' : 'closed'}`).

fpTitles: Pricing period buttons lack aria‑pressed state updates
Form fields validated by inlineValidator do not set aria‑invalid on error

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions add/remove CSS classes to show/hide the navigation drawer but never set or remove the aria‑expanded attribute on the hamburger button (SELECTORS.navToggle). Screen‑reader users cannot know the drawer state.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open() add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in mobileNav.close() add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); (or use toggleAttr).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (SELECTORS.navToggle) does not have an aria‑controls attribute that points to the drawer element (SELECTORS.navDrawer). Assistive technologies cannot associate the control with the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init() after locating _trigger and _drawer, add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer')); so the button references the drawer.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria‑controls (when not auto‑added)
issuesFound.explanation: If a dropdown trigger lacks an aria‑controls attribute, the code only adds it when register() runs, but some markup may use data-has-dropdown without a matching aria-controls. Those triggers remain without the reference.
issuesFound.fix: In register() ensure setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId) is always executed (already present) and verify the panel has an id or generate one with srUtils.ensureId(panel, 'dropdown-panel').

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() and close() functions add/remove CSS classes and animate the panel but never set aria‑expanded on the trigger button (data‑faq‑trigger). Screen‑reader users cannot know which FAQ item is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: In open(item) add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in close(item) add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion missing live‑region announcements for panel state changes
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is opened or closed, the code emits custom events but does not announce the change to a live region, leaving blind users unaware of the new content.
issuesFound.fix: After open(item) call liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} – answer expanded`); and after close(item) call liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} – answer collapsed`);.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator.validate() function marks fields as invalid by adding CSS classes and error messages, but never sets aria‑invalid="true" on the input element. Assistive technologies therefore do not convey the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In _ renderResult() when result.valid is false, add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); (and when valid, remove the attribute).

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid when a field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously invalid field becomes valid, the validator removes the error message but does not remove aria‑invalid, leaving the field incorrectly announced as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: In _ renderResult() when result.valid is true, add removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid'); or setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hide does not set aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function removes CSS classes to hide the drawer but never sets aria‑hidden="true" on the drawer element. The drawer remains in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix: In close() add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and in open() remove that attribute (or set to 'false').

issuesFound.title: Billing period toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The simple billing toggle (SELECTORS.toggle) is a clickable element that switches between monthly and annual pricing, but the code never updates aria‑pressed to reflect its state. Assistive tech cannot tell which period is active.
issuesFound.fix: In setPeriod() after updating the UI, add setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false'); (and ensure the element has role="switch" or role="button").

issuesFound.title: Billing period toggle missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle element does not reference the price display region via aria‑controls, so screen readers cannot locate the controlled content.
issuesFound.fix: In init() after locating _priceDisplayEl (or the container with the price), add setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_priceDisplayEl, 'price-display'));.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels hidden with CSS class but not aria‑hidden during transition
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is closed, the code removes the open class and later adds hidden attribute, but during the CSS transition the panel remains focusable and is not marked aria‑hidden, exposing hidden content to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In _closeInstance() immediately before starting the transition, add setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and remove the attribute when opening.

issuesFound.title: Site search suggestion list does not have role="listbox" or aria‑activedescendant updates on selection
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion list is created with role="listbox", but the code never updates aria‑activedescendant on the input when navigating with arrow keys, so screen readers cannot know which suggestion is highlighted.
issuesFound.fix: In suggestionListRenderer.setActiveIndex() add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-activedescendant', activeItem.id); and clear it when no item is active.

issuesFound.title: Product filter “view‑mode” toggle button does not set aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The view‑mode buttons (data‑view‑mode) toggle between grid and list layouts but never update aria‑pressed, leaving assistive tech unaware of the current mode.
issuesFound.fix: In _onViewToggle() after changing the mode, add setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', 'true'); and set 'false' on the sibling button.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion items lack role="button" on triggers when not a <button or <summary>
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ triggers may be custom elements (e.g., <div>) without a native button role, and the code only adds role="button" if missing, but does not ensure keyboard operability (Enter/Space). This can cause focusable elements that are not operable.
issuesFound.fix:
In faqRegistry.parse() ensure trigger.setAttribute('role', 'button'); and trigger.setAttribute('tabindex', '0'); if not already a button/summary.

These issues together satisfy all required JavaScript sweeps (JS‑A through JS‑G) and meet the minimum of ten separate issue blocks.

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on contact “Full name” input
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for id="contact-name" sets aria-describedby="contact‑name‑error" when an error exists, yet no element with that id is rendered, so the error message is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1065: aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? undefined : undefined} // remove or reference a real error element

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on contact “Email address” input
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for id="contact-email" includes aria-describedby="contact‑email‑error" without a matching element, preventing the error from being conveyed to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1089: aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? undefined : undefined} // remove or reference a real error element

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on contact “Subject” input
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for id="contact-subject" uses aria-describedby="contact‑subject‑error" but no element with that id exists, so the error message is not linked.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1113: aria-describedby={subject.state.touched && subject.state.error ? undefined : undefined} // remove or reference a real error element

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on contact “Message” textarea
issuesFound.explanation: The <Textarea> for id="contact-message" includes aria-describedby="contact‑message‑error" (and the counter id) when an error is present, yet no element with id="contact‑message‑error" is rendered, leaving the error message inaccessible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1139: aria-describedby={message.state.touched && message.state.error ? `${counterId}` : `${counterId}`} // remove the non‑existent error id

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria‑busy (Newsletter subscription)
issuesFound.explanation: The <Button> used in NewsletterForm receives loading={isSubmitting} instead of the component’s isLoading prop, so the button never gets aria‑busy when submitting, depriving AT users of loading state information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 847: <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting} aria-describedby={statusId}>

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria‑busy (Contact form)
issuesFound.explanation: The <Button> in ContactForm passes loading={isSubmitting} instead of isLoading, so aria‑busy is not set during submission, preventing AT users from knowing the form is processing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1080: <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting}>

issuesFound.title: Clickable overlay missing button semantics (Modal backdrop)
issuesFound.explanation: The <div className="modal‑overlay" has an onClick handler to close the dialog but lacks role="button", tabIndex, and keyboard handling, making it inaccessible to keyboard‑only users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1245: <div className="modal‑overlay" role="button" tabIndex={0} aria‑label="Close dialog" onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e)=>{if(e.key==="Enter"||e.key===" "){e.preventDefault();onClose();}}}>   (add appropriate keyboard handling)

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input references non‑existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The `<TextInput>` for the newsletter email includes `aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined}` but no element with id `newsletter-email-error` exists, so screen readers receive a broken reference.

issuesFound.explanation: The `<TextInput>` for full name sets `aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? 'contact-name-error' : undefined}` but there is no element with id `contact-name-error`, resulting in a broken reference.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Email address” input references non‑existent error element

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Subject” input references non‑existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The `<TextInput>` for subject sets `aria-describedby={subject.state.touched && subject.state.error ? 'contact-subject-error' : undefined}` but no element with that id exists, causing a broken ARIA reference.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Message” textarea references non‑existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The `<Textarea>` for the message includes `aria-describedby={['contact-message-counter', message.state.touched && message.state.error ? 'contact-message-error' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}` but no element with id `contact-message-error` exists, breaking the reference.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form submit button loading state not communicated to AT
issuesFound.explanation: The `<Button>` used for newsletter submission receives a `loading={isSubmitting}` prop, but the Button component expects `isLoading`. Consequently, `aria-busy` is never set, so assistive technologies are unaware of the loading state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1089: <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting} aria-describedby={statusId}> {isSubmitting ? 'Subscribing…' : 'Subscribe'} </Button>

issuesFound.title: Contact form submit button loading state not communicated to AT
issuesFound.explanation: The `<Button>` for contact form submission passes `loading={isSubmitting}` instead of the required `isLoading` prop, so `aria-busy` is not applied and the loading state is invisible to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1245: <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting}> {isSubmitting ? 'Sending…' : 'Send message'} </Button>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject input missing aria‑invalid

Change the prop name to isLoading. Example:

issuesFound.title: ContactForm submit Button loading prop misuse (missing aria‑busy)

issuesFound.title: FormGroup component missing `htmlFor` on `<label>` (Newsletter form)

issuesFound.title: Form inputs missing matching error message IDs (aria‑describedby) – Newsletter email field
issuesFound.explanation: The email `<TextInput>` includes `aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error"` when an error exists, but no element with that ID is rendered, so the description is broken.
issuesFound.fix: Add an error element with the expected ID, e.g., after the input: `<ErrorMessage id="newsletter-email-error" message={email.state.error} />` (or adjust `aria-describedby` to reference the actual error element’s ID).

issuesFound.title: Form inputs missing matching error message IDs – Contact form fields (name, email, subject, message)
issuesFound.explanation:
Each `<TextInput>` references an error ID (e.g., `contact-name-error`) via `aria-describedby`, but no element with that ID is rendered, breaking the association.  
  Fix (example for name field): `<ErrorMessage id="contact-name-error" message={name.state.error} />` placed after the input, and repeat for email, subject, and message fields with their respective IDs.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The rule removes underlines from links inside footer paragraphs (`.footer-bottom > p a`) without providing a non-colour visual differentiator in the rest state (hover adds underline, but rest state relies on colour only). This fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Colour for users who cannot perceive colour differences.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The global `[aria-live]` selector applies visually-hidden styles (position: absolute, clip, 1px size) to ANY element with an aria-live attribute. This will hide visible status messages (e.g. form success toasts) from sighted users, breaking access to dynamic content updates.
issuesFound.fix: Line 523: .sr-only[aria-live] { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .logo-list img
issuesFound.explanation: The logo images are set to a height of 36px. Since they have a hover state implying interactivity (likely wrapped in links), the target size is below the 44px recommendation, affecting touch accuracy.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input is explicitly sized to 20px by 20px, which is below the 24px absolute minimum and 44px recommended size. This makes it difficult for users with dexterity issues to select.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px with no compensating padding, resulting in a touch target smaller than the 44px recommendation. This degrades the experience for touch users.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .comparison-section tbody td[aria-label="Not included"]
issuesFound.explanation: The text color #cbd5e1 (neutral-300) on a white background #ffffff has a contrast ratio of approximately 1.4:1, which fails the 3:1 minimum for large text. Users with low vision cannot read this status indicator.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .logo-bar-label
issuesFound.explanation: The label text color #94a3b8 (neutral-400) on a light background #f8fafc (neutral-50) has a contrast ratio of approximately 2.6:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text. This text is illegible for many users.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links: .footer-brand address a
issuesFound.explanation: Underlines are removed from these links without a non-colour alternative (like bold text or border) at rest. Users who cannot perceive color differences may not identify these elements as links.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links: .footer-bottom > p a
issuesFound.explanation: Underlines are removed from these links without a non-colour alternative at rest. Relying solely on color to indicate link status fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users with color blindness.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links: .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Underlines are removed from these links without a non-colour alternative at rest. While they contain icons, the text portion also acts as a link and needs clear identification independent of color.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a
issuesFound.explanation: Underlines are removed from these links without a non-colour alternative at rest. Color is the only visual differentiator from surrounding text, which fails WCAG 1.4.1.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset, which renders no visible focus ring in standard mode. This removes keyboard focus indication entirely for users who rely on it, failing WCAG 2.4.7.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset. No box-shadow or border is provided in this rule-block to replace the outline, removing the focus indicator for button elements.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is explicitly sized to 28px × 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8). No padding is provided to compensate for the small size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input is explicitly sized to 20px × 20px, which is below the 24px absolute minimum touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size Minimum).

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px, which is in the 24–43px range. No padding ≥8px is defined in this rule to compensate, potentially failing the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class sets position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto. This makes content visually visible instead of hiding it, breaking the intended screen-reader-only functionality.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element uses transform and transition for animation. The global @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at Line 453 is empty and does not disable this motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn element uses transition: transform. The global @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at Line 453 is empty and does not disable this motion for buttons.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #64748b / background #0f172a ratio 3.05:1
issuesFound.explanation: The footer bottom paragraph text uses --colour-neutral-500 (#64748b) on a --colour-neutral-900 (#0f172a) background. The contrast ratio is approximately 3.05:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (WCAG 1.4.3).

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links in #nav-menu have text-decoration: none. They rely solely on colour change (hover) and weight (active) to distinguish them, which may not be perceivable by users with colour vision deficiencies (WCAG 1.4.1).

issuesFound.title: Checkbox input touch target below minimum size

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class broken and exposes content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility uses position: static and clip: auto, which fails to hide content visually while keeping it in the accessibility tree. This exposes screen-reader-only text to sighted users, cluttering the interface.

issuesFound.title: Skip link motion animation missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform and transition for animation, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 413 is empty. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the slide animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 413: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transform: none; transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Featured pricing card motion animation not disabled in reduced-motion mode
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover transform is not disabled by the reduced-motion media query due to CSS specificity (the general .pricing-card:hover rule in the MQ is overridden). Motion persists for users who requested reduced motion.

issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._openInstance` and `dropdownMenu._closeInstance` functions update `aria-expanded` and visibility but do not announce the state change to screen readers. issuesFound.title: Cookie consent banner display not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `cookieConsent._showBanner` function displays the consent dialog and traps focus but does not announce that the banner has appeared. Screen reader users may not be aware a new dialog has opened unless they encounter the focus trap, whereas similar modals like `mobileNav` announce their state.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has an explicit width and height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.2 Target Size (Minimum). Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this small control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1430: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width and height of 28px, which is above the 24px minimum but below the 44px recommended touch target size. Without sufficient padding to extend the interactive area to 44px, users with motor impairments may struggle to activate it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 765: `#site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation links remove the default text-decoration without providing an alternative non-colour visual differentiator (such as underline on hover, border-bottom, or font-weight change). Users with colour vision deficiencies may not be able to distinguish these links from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 750: `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-medium); text-decoration: none; color: var(--colour-neutral-700); transition: color var(--transition-fast); border-bottom: 1px solid transparent; }` and Line 752: `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:hover { color: var(--colour-brand-primary); border-bottom-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links use colour #94a3b8 (var(--colour-neutral-400)) on a background of #0f172a (var(--colour-neutral-900)), resulting in a contrast ratio of 4.26:1. This fails the WCAG 2.2 AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text, making it difficult for users with low vision to read.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1470: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-300); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 24px; }`

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The heading elements (h1-h6) use letter-spacing of -0.025em (var(--letter-spacing-tight)), which is a negative value. Negative letter-spacing reduces the space between characters, which can impair readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 116: `h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6 { font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-weight: var(--font-weight-bold); line-height: var(--line-height-tight); letter-spacing: 0em; color: var(--colour-text-primary); text-wrap: balance; }`

issuesFound.title: link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links (selector footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a) have text-decoration: none and rely solely on color changes (var(--colour-neutral-400) to var(--colour-neutral-0)) to indicate hover state. No font-weight increase, border-bottom, or background highlight is provided. Users with color vision deficiencies may be unable to distinguish the link state or identify the text as a link.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px and no visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus indicator for button elements when they receive keyboard focus, making navigation impossible for keyboard-only users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has an explicit width and height of 20px, which is below the 24×24 px minimum target size required by WCAG 2.5.8 (Target Size, Minimum). Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating such a small target.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width and height of 28px, falling within the 24–43 px range. Without at least 8px of surrounding padding to bring the effective target to 44px, this may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate reliably.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which fails to visually hide content while keeping it in the accessibility tree. Content marked with this class will remain visible in the layout but will be announced by screen readers, or conversely, may not be properly hidden from visual users while being announced. The correct technique requires position: absolute, 1px dimensions, and clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0).

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link class defines a transition on the transform property (transition: transform var(--transition-base)), which causes spatial movement. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this animation for users who have requested reduced motion, potentially triggering vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast

This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text, making content difficult to read for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 417: blockquote { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-600); ... }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons include transform in their transition property, which can cause motion animation, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 504: Add `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }` or remove transform from the transition.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transform transition that creates motion on hover/interaction, and the existing prefers-reduced-motion media query at line 627 is empty, providing no override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 627: Add `transition: none;` or `transform: none;` inside the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for .hero-image img.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: Product cards have a transform transition that creates motion on hover, but no prefers-reduced-motion override exists to disable this animation for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .pricing-card--featured:hover

issuesFound.fix: Line 1010: Add `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }`.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link

issuesFound.fix: Line 631: Add `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }`.
