issuesFound.explanation: The button component has a minimum height of 32px, which is below the recommended 44px. The vertical padding of 7.5px (0.625rem) is less than 8px and does not compensate to reach the recommended touch target size. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 248: .btn { ... min-height: 44px; ... }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm (min-height: 20px)
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant has a minimum height of 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum of 24px and far below the recommended 44px. This creates a significant barrier for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 309: .btn-sm { ... min-height: 44px; ... }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon (min-width: 20px, min-height: 20px)
issuesFound.explanation: Icon-only buttons have a minimum size of 20px × 20px, which is below the 24px minimum and 44px recommended target size. This makes activation difficult for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 322: .btn-icon { ... min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; ... }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .nav-toggle (min-height: 28px, min-width: 28px)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle has a size of 28px with only 6px padding, resulting in a touch target below the recommended 44px. Users may accidentally activate adjacent elements or miss the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 333: .nav-toggle { ... min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; ... }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #search-input (height: 28px)
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has a fixed height of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target size. While padding is 8px, the total height remains 28px due to border-box sizing, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to tap accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 365: #search-input { ... height: 44px; ... }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #site-search button[type="submit"] (height: 28px, width: 28px)
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has dimensions of 28px × 28px with zero padding, creating a touch target well below the recommended 44px. This poses accessibility barriers for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 379: #site-search button[type="submit"] { ... width: 44px; height: 44px; ... }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] (width: 20px, height: 20px, min-height: 20px)
issuesFound.explanation: Checkboxes have a fixed size of 20px × 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate these small targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 901: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { ... width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; ... }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a (min-height: 24px)
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have a minimum height of 24px, meeting the absolute minimum but falling short of the recommended 44px touch target size. The lack of padding means the clickable area remains at 24px.
issuesFound.fix: Line 919: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { ... min-height: 44px; padding-block: 10px; ... }

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and clip: auto, which makes the content visible to sighted users instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. Additionally, it lacks the necessary properties to remove content from the visual layout (such as position: absolute and clip: rect(0,0,0,0)), effectively breaking the utility's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 212: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button component includes transform in its transition property (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)), which can cause spatial movement. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this transition for users who experience vestibular disorders or motion sensitivity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 248: (add to existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block or create new) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: This specific button override includes transform in its transition without a prefers-reduced-motion override, which can trigger motion sensitivity issues. The existing @media blocks do not target this specific selector's transition.
issuesFound.fix: Line 369: (add to existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words. This negative spacing can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and may cause words to overlap in some font rendering scenarios.
issuesFound.fix: Line 122: body { ... word-spacing: normal; ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .section-header p (foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff / ratio 2.3:1)
issuesFound.explanation: The section header paragraph uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on a white background (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1. This fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text (4.5:1), making content difficult to read for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 260: .section-header p { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-600); ... } (or darker colour meeting 4.5:1)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .input-hint (foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff / ratio 2.3:1)
issuesFound.explanation: Input hints use --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on a white background, providing only 2.3:1 contrast. This fails WCAG AA standards and makes helper text difficult to read for users with visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 898: .input-hint { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-600); ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: footer[role="contentinfo"] (foreground #cbd5e1 / background #555555 / ratio 1.8:1)
issuesFound.explanation: The footer uses --colour-neutral-300 (#cbd5e1) text on --colour-neutral-900 (#555555) background, resulting in approximately 1.8:1 contrast. This fails WCAG AA requirements (4.5:1 for normal text), making footer content illegible for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 913: footer[role="contentinfo"] { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-0); ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .footer-brand > p (foreground #94a3b8 / background #555555 / ratio 2.0:1)
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand paragraphs use --colour-neutral-400 (#94a3b8) on --colour-neutral-900 (#555555), providing only 2.0:1 contrast. This fails WCAG AA standards for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 917: .footer-brand > p { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-200); ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .footer-brand address (foreground #64748b / background #555555 / ratio 1.4:1)
issuesFound.explanation: The address text uses --colour-neutral-500 (#64748b) on --colour-neutral-900 (#555555), resulting in approximately 1.4:1 contrast. This is well below the 4.5:1 requirement and makes contact information unreadable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 919: .footer-brand address { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-300); ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a (foreground #94a3b8 / background #555555 / ratio 2.0:1)
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links use --colour-neutral-400 (#94a3b8) on --colour-neutral-900 (#555555), providing only 2.0:1 contrast. This fails WCAG AA and makes navigation links difficult to distinguish.
issuesFound.fix: Line 919: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-200); ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .footer-bottom > p (foreground #64748b / background #555555 / ratio 1.4:1)
issuesFound.explanation: Footer copyright text uses --colour-neutral-500 (#64748b) on dark background (#555555), resulting in 1.4:1 contrast. This fails WCAG AA requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 925: .footer-bottom > p { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-300); ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .footer-bottom > p a (foreground #94a3b8 / background #555555 / ratio 2.0:1)
issuesFound.explanation: Links in the footer bottom use --colour-neutral-400 (#94a3b8) on #555555, providing only 2.0:1 contrast. This fails WCAG AA standards.
issuesFound.fix: Line 927: .footer-bottom > p a { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-200); ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .social-links a (foreground #94a3b8 / background #555555 / ratio 2.0:1)
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links use --colour-neutral-400 (#94a3b8) on dark background, resulting in 2.0:1 contrast. This fails WCAG AA requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 929: .social-links a { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-200); ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .product-tagline (foreground #5588bb / background #ffffff / ratio 3.9:1)
issuesFound.explanation: Product taglines use --colour-brand-primary (#5588bb) on white background, providing approximately 3.9:1 contrast. This is below the 4.5:1 required for normal text (font-size: 0.875rem/14px), making category labels difficult to read.
issuesFound.fix: Line 594: .product-tagline { ... color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); ... } (darker shade) or adjust to meet 4.5:1

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .comparison-section tbody td[aria-label="Not included"], .comparison-section tbody td[aria-label="Not available"] (foreground #cbd5e1 / background #ffffff / ratio 1.3:1)
issuesFound.explanation: Table cells indicating "Not included" use --colour-neutral-300 (#cbd5e1) on white background (and alternating --colour-neutral-50 #f1f5f9), providing only 1.3:1 contrast. This fails WCAG AA and makes it difficult to distinguish between included and excluded features.
issuesFound.fix: Line 771: .comparison-section tbody td[aria-label="Not included"], .comparison-section tbody td[aria-label="Not available"] { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-500); ... }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links rely solely on color changes (from --colour-neutral-700 to --colour-brand-primary) to indicate they are links. Without underlines or other non-color visual differentiators, users with color vision deficiencies cannot distinguish links from regular text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 349: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a { ... text-decoration: underline; ... } OR add border-bottom or background change on hover/focus that is not color-only

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links rely only on color changes for identification. With low contrast already present and no underline or border, users with color vision deficiencies cannot identify these as interactive links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 919: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { ... text-decoration: underline; ... }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links in the footer rely solely on color to indicate they are links. No underline, border, or background change is provided, failing SC 1.4.1 for users with color vision deficiencies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 929: .social-links a { ... text-decoration: underline; ... }

issuesFound.title: SUPPORTS.dom property undefined causing focus trap failure
issuesFound.explanation: The code references SUPPORTS.dom at lines 2343 and 2510, but the SUPPORTS object (defined around line 657) does not contain a 'dom' property. This evaluates to undefined (falsy), preventing the focus trap event listeners from being attached in createFocusTrap and causing keyboardNavigationGuard to exit early. This breaks keyboard accessibility for modal dialogs and navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 657: Add `dom: typeof document !== 'undefined',` to the SUPPORTS object freeze.

issuesFound.title: File upload validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: In the fileUploadField module, the _validateFile function announces validation errors via liveRegion but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input element when validation fails. Screen reader users will hear the error but the input will not be marked as invalid in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5338: After `liveRegion.announceAssertive(...)` in the validation failure block, add: `setAttr(binding.inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`. In the success path (where file is added), add: `setAttr(binding.inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module's _openInstance and _closeInstance functions update aria-expanded and CSS classes but do not announce the state change via the liveRegion. Users navigating with screen readers will not be informed when the dropdown panel opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2844: In _openInstance, after `emit(...)`, add: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');`. Line 2860: In _closeInstance, after `emit(...)`, add: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header hide/show not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The stickyHeader module toggles the header visibility (hiding on scroll down, showing on scroll up) by adding/removing CSS classes, but it does not announce this state change via a live region. Screen reader users may not realize the header has disappeared or reappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2975: In _onScroll, after `addClass(_header, CSS.headerHidden)` add: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.headerHidden || 'Header hidden');`. After `removeClass(_header, CSS.headerHidden)` add: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.headerShown || 'Header shown');`.

issuesFound.title: Viewport reveal elements not hidden from accessibility tree initially
issuesFound.explanation: The viewportReveal module selects elements with [data-reveal] and animates them when they enter the viewport. If these elements are initially hidden via CSS opacity:0 only, they remain in the accessibility tree. The init function does not set aria-hidden="true" on these elements before they are revealed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5555: In the init function, after `qsa(SELECTOR).forEach(el => {`, add: `setAttr(el, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. Then in the _reveal function (line ~5540), add: `removeAttr(el, 'aria-hidden');` before or when adding the VISIBLE_CLS.

issuesFound.title: Stat counter updates not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The statCounterAnimator module animates number counters by updating textContent, but it does not ensure the elements have aria-live. Screen reader users will not hear the final count values as they update.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5580: In the _animate function, after `if (el.getAttribute('data-stat-animated') === 'true') return;`, add: `if (!el.getAttribute('aria-live')) setAttr(el, 'aria-live', 'polite');`.

issuesFound.title: Contact form fields missing autocomplete attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The CONTACT_FIELDS configuration for the contact form includes fields for full_name, email, etc., but the inlineValidator binding does not set the autocomplete attribute on these fields. This prevents browsers from assisting users with autofill for personal data fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4690: Add `autocomplete` property to each field config (e.g., `autocomplete: 'name'` for full_name, `autocomplete: 'email'` for email). Then in the init function (around line 5060), after binding validation, add: `if (cfg.autocomplete) field.setAttribute('autocomplete', cfg.autocomplete);`.

issuesFound.title: Fade animations do not manage aria-hidden state
issuesFound.explanation: The fadeOut and fadeIn animation helpers manipulate element opacity but do not set aria-hidden. During fadeOut, the element remains in the accessibility tree despite being visually transparent. This affects the cookieConsent banner and other components using these utilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1620: In fadeOut, after `if (!isElement(el)) return;`, add: `setAttr(el, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. Line 1645: In fadeIn, after `if (!isElement(el)) return;`, add: `removeAttr(el, 'aria-hidden');`.

issuesFound.title: Theme switcher click handler not ensuring button accessibility
issuesFound.explanation: The themeSwitcher module attaches a click listener to [data-theme-toggle] elements but does not verify they are native buttons or have role="button" and tabindex="0". If the HTML uses divs or spans, they will be inaccessible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5788: Inside the _onThemeClick handler (or in the init function where listeners are attached), after getting `btn`, add: `if (btn.tagName !== 'BUTTON') { setAttr(btn, 'role', 'button'); if (!btn.hasAttribute('tabindex')) setAttr(btn, 'tabindex', '0'); }`.

issuesFound.title: Theme switcher toggle lacks keyboard activation support
issuesFound.explanation: The themeSwitcher attaches a click handler to [data-theme-toggle] elements to cycle themes. If these elements are not native <button> tags (e.g., <div> or <span>), they will not respond to Enter or Space key presses, making them inaccessible to keyboard users. The code does not attach keydown handlers to support non-button elements.

issuesFound.title: Product view-mode toggle lacks keyboard activation support
issuesFound.explanation: The view-mode toggle (grid/list) uses event delegation on [data-view-mode] elements with only a click handler. If these are not native <button> elements, keyboard users cannot activate them with Enter or Space.

issuesFound.title: Roving tabindex pattern lacks Enter/Space activation
issuesFound.explanation: The createRovingTabindex utility manages focus for composite widgets (filter tabs, pricing period buttons) but only handles arrow keys, Home, and End. It does not handle Enter or Space to activate the focused item when activateOnFocus is false (default), preventing keyboard users from selecting items that are not native buttons.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not removed from accessibility tree when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When closing the mobile nav drawer, the code removes the 'is-open' CSS class but does not set aria-hidden="true". If the CSS uses off-screen positioning (transform: translateX, negative margins) rather than display:none, the drawer remains in the accessibility tree, allowing screen reader users to navigate to hidden content.
issuesFound.fix:
Line: Inside close function, after removeClass:
  setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Sticky header not removed from accessibility tree when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the sticky header is hidden on scroll-down, it adds a CSS class but does not set aria-hidden="true". If the CSS hides the header using transform: translateY(-100%) or similar off-screen techniques while maintaining display:block, the header remains accessible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line: Inside hide function, after addClass:
  setAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden', 'true');

fpTitles: Theme switcher toggle lacks keyboard activation support
Product view-mode toggle lacks keyboard activation support
Roving tabindex pattern lacks Enter/Space activation
Mobile navigation drawer not removed from accessibility tree when hidden
Sticky header not removed from accessibility tree when hidden

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion custom trigger missing keyboard activation
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item trigger is not a native `<button>` or `<summary>` element, the code assigns `role="button"` and `tabindex="0"` but fails to attach a `keydown` event listener to handle `Enter` and `Space` activation. Keyboard-only users cannot expand or collapse the FAQ panel because the delegated click handler on the container is not triggered by keyboard events on the custom button.
issuesFound.fix: Add keydown listener for Enter/Space on the trigger element:

issuesFound.title: Form error message has conflicting ARIA live attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The dynamically created error message element is initialized with both `role="alert"` (which implies `aria-live="assertive"`) and `aria-live="polite"`. This creates conflicting instructions for assistive technologies, potentially causing the error to be announced inconsistently or with the wrong priority.
issuesFound.fix: Remove the `aria-live` attribute when `role="alert"` is present:

fpTitles: FAQ accordion custom trigger missing keyboard activation
Form error message has conflicting ARIA live attributes

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer open function adds CSS classes to show the drawer but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2458: After `_lockScroll();` add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu open function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes to show the panel, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing when the dropdown is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2570: After `requestAnimationFrame(() => addClass(inst.panel, CSS.open));` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open function animates the panel open and adds CSS classes, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the question button. The close function sets it to false, resulting in an asymmetrical state where the expanded state is never communicated to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3760: After `addClass(item.el, CSS.open);` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab synchronization function toggles visual active classes but does not update the aria-pressed attribute on the buttons. Since these buttons act as toggles for category filters, the pressed state must be communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3129: After `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput validation missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When the discount code validation fails, the code displays an error message but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the input field. When validation succeeds, it does not clear aria-invalid. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3658: Inside the else block (invalid result), add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`. Line 3663: Inside the if (result.valid) block, add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu open and close functions emit custom DOM events but do not write to an aria-live region. Users navigating with screen readers receive no audible confirmation when the menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2572: After setting aria-expanded, add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');`. Line 2590: In _closeInstance, after setting aria-expanded to false, add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation close function removes CSS classes but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element. If the CSS does not use display:none when closed, the drawer remains in the accessibility tree and can be reached by screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2459: In open function, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');`. Line 2474: In close function, after `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`

fpTitles: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the function adds CSS classes and emits events but fails to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true". Screen reader users cannot perceive that the navigation region is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2027: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the `_open = true;` assignment.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function reveals the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but it never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, violating the disclosure pattern required by WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2220: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function expands the FAQ answer panel using animateHeight and CSS classes but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, leaving assistive technology users unaware of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3354: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function adds the error message element to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers cannot convey the error state to users, violating WCAG 3.3.1 and 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1285: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the function after the guard clause.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not activate focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation drawer opens but focus is not trapped within it; keyboard users can tab out of the drawer into the background page while it remains visually open, violating WCAG 2.4.3 (focus order) and 2.4.7 (focus visible).

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader scroll handler does not announce state changes
issuesFound.explanation: The header hides and shows automatically based on scroll direction, but this state change is not announced via a live region, potentially confusing screen reader users who may not realize the navigation has disappeared or reappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2380: Inside the `if (movingDown && !_hidden)` block, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.headerHidden || 'Navigation header hidden');` and inside the `else if (!movingDown && _hidden)` block, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.headerShown || 'Navigation header shown');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion._onKeydown() missing Escape handler
issuesFound.explanation: When keyboard focus is on an expanded FAQ trigger, pressing Escape does not collapse the panel and return focus to the trigger, violating the standard accordion keyboard interaction pattern (WCAG 2.1.1).

fpTitles: faqAccordion._onKeydown() missing Escape handler

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function toggles the mobile navigation drawer visually and sets _open = true, but never updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true". Screen readers will continue to report the menu as collapsed even after it opens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1378: After `_open = true;` add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the function sets inst.open = true and shows the panel, but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close counterpart correctly sets it to "false", leaving the state out of sync.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1512: After `inst.open = true;` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding an FAQ item sets item.open = true and removes the hidden attribute from the panel, but does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". The close() function sets it to "false", so the state is only correct when closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2927: After `item.open = true;` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() omits aria-invalid attribute

issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons maintain a selected state (value) but lack aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot identify whether monthly or annual billing is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix:
Line billing-monthly button: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>
  Fix: Line billing-annual button: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active navigation link is visually indicated by a CSS class but lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot identify which page is the current page in the navigation.
issuesFound.fix: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks nav missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation element containing skip links lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby. When multiple nav elements exist on the page, users cannot distinguish this landmark's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: <nav aria-label="Skip navigation">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav nav missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The primary navigation landmark lacks an aria-label. With multiple navigation regions present, screen reader users cannot differentiate this from other navigation blocks.
issuesFound.fix: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav nav missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation panel contains a nav element without an aria-label, making it indistinguishable from other navigation landmarks on the page.
issuesFound.fix: <nav className="mobile-nav__body" aria-label="Mobile navigation">

issuesFound.title: HeroSection section missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section lacks aria-labelledby or aria-label. As a major page region, it should be identifiable by screen reader users navigating by landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">

issuesFound.title: StatsBar section missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics bar section contains a visually hidden heading but the section itself lacks aria-labelledby, preventing screen reader users from identifying this landmark region.
issuesFound.fix: <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing plan card uses an article element but lacks aria-labelledby referencing the plan name heading. Screen reader users cannot identify the card's subject when navigating by article regions.
issuesFound.fix: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: HeroSection CTAs group missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The group of call-to-action buttons lacks an aria-label. As a distinct group of related controls, it should be labelled to provide context for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: <div className="hero-section__ctas" aria-label="Call to action links">

issuesFound.title: HeroSection trust badges group missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The trust badge row contains multiple certification indicators but lacks an aria-label. Screen reader users encounter an unlabelled group of images and text.
issuesFound.fix: <div className="hero-section__trust" aria-label="Trust indicators">

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component accepts an isLoading state but does not apply aria-busy to the button element. Screen reader users are not notified when the button is in a loading state.
issuesFound.fix: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: Button spinner missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The loading spinner span is decorative but lacks aria-hidden="true". Screen reader users may encounter redundant "Loading" announcements alongside the button's aria-busy state.
issuesFound.fix: <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar cloned list missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated list used for visual marquee looping lacks aria-hidden="true". Screen reader users hear the integration list twice unnecessarily.
issuesFound.fix: <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: StarRating decorative stars missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Individual star symbols are decorative within a container that has an aria-label, but they lack aria-hidden. Screen reader users may hear "star" characters in addition to the rating announcement.
issuesFound.fix: {stars.map((filled, i) => ({filled ? '★' : '☆'}))}

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk marker for required fields is not hidden from assistive technology, causing screen readers to announce "star" in addition to the "required" text that follows.
issuesFound.fix: *
